Title: Drought-responsive genes, late embryogenesis abundant group3 (LEA3) and vicinal oxygen chelate, function in lipid accumulation in Brassica napus and Arabidopsis mainly via enhancing photosynthetic efficiency and reducing ROS

Common_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: AtLEA3: AT1G02820; BnLEA3.1: KP315905; BnLEA3.2: KP315906; BnLEA3.3: KP315907; BnLEA3.4: KP315908
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major storage lipid and economic product in oil crops
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes seed oil accumulation and drought tolerance; increases seed size and thousand-seed weight
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Overexpression of AtLEA3 and multiple BnLEA3 copies increased seed oil content in Arabidopsis and B. napus under normal and drought conditions. 2. atlea3 mutant and BnLEA-RNAi lines showed reduced oil content, reduced seed size/weight, and lower drought tolerance. 3. AtLEA3 complementation rescued mutant oil content and drought-related phenotypes. 4. Transcriptome and physiological analyses indicated LEA3 mainly enhances lipid accumulation via improved photosynthetic efficiency, upregulation of fatty-acid biosynthesis genes, and reduction of lipid oxidation/degradation.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Selected BnLEA3 copies showed relatively stable and higher expression in seeds and leaves during drought based on previous studies
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Differential effects analyzed in leaves and siliques/seeds under short- and long-term drought
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression; T-DNA mutant analysis; RNAi; complementation; qRT-PCR; RNA-seq; oil content and fatty-acid profiling; physiological assays under drought
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: LACS9; LOX2; LIPASE1; acyl carrier proteins; lipid transfer proteins; SnRK-related components (transcript-level association)
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Drought stress; short-term PEG treatment; long-term water withholding
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: LEA3 is not a catalytic pathway enzyme or TF; it affects lipid accumulation mainly indirectly through photosynthesis, membrane stability, and suppression of fatty-acid degradation/lipid oxidation under drought. Hybrids with VOC-OE showed reinforced effects.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious morphological malformation reported in OE seeds; NA
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for improving oil yield and drought tolerance simultaneously in oil crops; can be stacked with VOC for stronger effects; seed-specific promoter may improve seed traits effectively

Common_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: AtVOC: AT1G07645; BnVOC1: KP315909; BnVOC2: KP315910; BnVOC3: KP315911; BnVOC4: KP315912
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major storage lipid and economic product in oil crops
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Vicinal oxygen chelate superfamily; glyoxalase I-related VOC protein
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes seed oil accumulation and drought tolerance mainly by reducing ROS, MG toxicity, and fatty-acid degradation; increases seed size and thousand-seed weight
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Overexpression of AtVOC and multiple BnVOC copies increased seed oil content in Arabidopsis and B. napus under normal and drought conditions. 2. AtVOC-RNAi and BnVOC-RNAi lines had reduced oil content, reduced seed size/weight, and lower drought tolerance. 3. VOC-OE lines showed reduced H2O2, MG, and MDA, altered GSH/GSSG status, and increased GLY I/GLY II activities. 4. Transcriptome data indicated VOC contributes to lipid accumulation mainly through suppressing fatty-acid degradation and affecting glyoxylate/dicarboxylate metabolism and photosynthetic/light-reaction related processes.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Selected BnVOC copies showed relatively stable and higher expression in seeds and leaves during drought based on previous studies
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Differential effects analyzed in leaves and siliques/seeds under short- and long-term drought
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression; RNAi; prokaryotic expression in E. coli for MG tolerance; qRT-PCR; RNA-seq; oil content and fatty-acid profiling; GLY I/GLY II and ROS-related enzyme assays; metabolite assays under drought
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: GLY I; GLY II; SOD1; LOX2; photosystem/light-reaction and lipid-metabolism genes (transcript-level association)
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Drought stress; short-term PEG treatment; long-term water withholding
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: VOC proteins are discussed as the main subfamily GLY I and function in MG detoxification/glyoxylate metabolism; despite enzyme-related activity, the focal paper studies VOC broadly as a drought-responsive functional gene affecting lipid accumulation rather than defining a classic lipid-biosynthetic catalytic step. Hybrids with LEA3-OE showed reinforced effects.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious morphological malformation reported in OE seeds; NA
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for engineering oil accumulation together with drought tolerance; stacking with LEA3 is supported by hybrid data; beneficial for reducing oxidative damage during drought
